text:
Deep within a forgotten cave, nestled between moss-covered walls and ancient stalactites, there lay a glimmer of light from a tarnished silver coin. The coin had once shone with the radiance of a star; it had seen the adventures of travelers who ventured into the depths seeking the legendary treasure of Arcturus. Unbeknownst to its previous companions, the coin had found a friend in a common mole named Mortimer in the passage ahead — a friend who had little interest in gold but who, in exchange for the coin's luminescence, would tell tales of the world above.

Mortimer had a curious mind, often asking questions of the minerals around him and the stories they whispered in the dust. One moonlit night, as the constellation Arcturus seemed to dance in the sky, Mortimer's storytelling began:

"In the tales passed down by the ancients, Arcturus was not just a star, but a beacon of bravery and wisdom. It guided
